critic_raw:
CRITIQUE (Round 1):

1. [ISSUE-A] PANEL_SPECIFIC_VS_GENERIC_THRESHOLDING: Calibration asymmetry.
2. [ISSUE-B] HOTSPOT_GENE_COMPOSITION_ADVANTAGE: 40% hotspot concentration.
3. [ISSUE-C] LIMITED_BASELINE_SET_FOR_MODERN_CLAIM: PolyPhen-2 only; modern tools absent.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (Round 1):
Domain calibration appropriate. Hotspot genes clinically relevant. PolyPhen-2 is the clinical comparator.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1: All open.

Round 2:
Critic [ISSUE-A]: AUROC is threshold-free; calibration asymmetry affects threshold-based deployment claims more than AUROC per se.
Defender [ISSUE-A]: Partially acknowledge — AUROC is threshold-free, but the threshold calibration issue matters for the specific deployment claim using the calibrated score.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-A — partially conceded; threshold point noted]

Critic [ISSUE-B]: 40% hotspot genes means benchmark may not represent the harder, non-hotspot portion of the panel. Performance breakdown by gene is missing.
Defender [ISSUE-B]: Concede — gene-level breakdown is needed to understand whether the 0.93 AUROC holds across the full panel.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-B — conceded]

Critic [ISSUE-C]: Claiming 'foundation-model representations outperform classical predictors' with only PolyPhen-2 as comparator is insufficient.
Defender [ISSUE-C]: Concede — modern comparators (AlphaMissense, ESM-1v) are needed for this claim.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-C — conceded]

Verdict: critique_wins.
